mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during periods of declining FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings contraction, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings revisions.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns when GDPC1 and RSAFS both grow, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive equity returns over 20 days when the economy operates below full capacity.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy forces central banks to maintain elevated rates, triggering negative short-term equity returns as real yields rise and discount rates widen.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and high-rate regime yields positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60 percent of observations, or the interaction effect reverses sign.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through top-line revenue growth.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 60-day SPX returns, or the signal generates negative returns during periods of payroll expansion exceeding 100000 monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 800000 units signal collapsing construction demand and weakening consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in over 50 percent of months where HOUST drops below 800, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and stable unemployment indicates productive efficiency gains without labor market stress, generating positive short-term equity returns as profit margins expand.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or the mechanism breaks down when unemployment rises concurrently with industrial production.
